questionText: Choose the correct result for this treasure-map fraction puzzle. A pirate chest card ends with 18/30, and it came from a smaller fraction in simplest form by multiplying the top and bottom by the same number one time. Which starting fraction and multiplier fit the card?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can generate equivalent fractions using multiplication and division
issues: [difficulty_mismatch, distractor_quality] | The item is labeled hard, but solving it is essentially simplifying 18/30 and matching the multiplier, which is only one to two direct steps and does not meet the stated hard criteria. | For a hard item, the distractors are not strong enough as misconception-based reasoning traps; two options are simply non-simplest equivalent fractions, making elimination straightforward. | Fix: Relabel the item as medium, or increase complexity by requiring students to evaluate multiple possible starting fractions/multipliers or justify why only one works. | Fix: Strengthen distractors so each reflects a realistic misconception, such as incorrect simplification, using a non-common factor, or mismatching the multiplier with only one part of the fraction.
